rulings.Case Title: THE ESTATE OF VICTOR CADIZ
rulings.Calendar Matter: ACCOUNTING PETITION RE: Accounting #1, Final Distribution
rulings.Rulings: Continued to 4/5/21. No earlier availability. Another "Amended First and Final Account," etc., was filed on 2/16/21, when it should have been the SECOND Amended pleading. No proof of service has been filed to reflect that notice of this new "Amended First and Final Account," etc., has been served on all interested parties. In any event, a pleading filed on 2/16/21 would not have allowed for proper service of notice for a hearing on 3/1/21. No account was submitted as part of this pleading. There is also no Summary of Account, as alleged in paragraph 15, which refers to Exhibit A, which exhibit was also referenced in paragraph 6 of the pleading as listing the creditors' claims. The list of creditors' claims still does not meet the requirements of CRC 7.403. The amounts of the two I&As mentioned in paragraph 9 of the pleading do not total the amount stated in paragraph 17 of the pleading, which latter amount was used to calculate the statutory compensation. The amounts shown on Exhibit A under "Creditors and expenses" reflect a balance of fees to the attorney, when no amounts were authorized to be paid yet. Probate Code Section 9202(b) also requires notice if an heir or beneficiary was previously so confined, not just currently. It appears that the creditor's claim filed by Joseph Cadiz on 6/3/20 was late (if it was, in fact, a proper claim); the executor does not address this or why she is going to pay a late filed claim that also does not appear to be a legitimate claim when only one entry among the services listed preceded the decedent's date of death. If the executor is proposing to share her statutory compensation with Joseph Cadiz, that must be clearly set forth. [Probate Code Section 10805.] No original bank statement has been filed. No statement by the attorney or the executor (whoever is retaining the original statement) has been filed to certify that he or she will retain the original(s) as set forth in LRSF 2.11 M.4. A SECOND AMENDED PETITION must be filed with the account attached unless the accounting is waived by all interested parties. The SECOND AMENDED PETITION must be filed in time to give notice at least 15 days before 4/5/21 to all interested parties, including any creditor who has not yet been paid. If the SECOND AMENDED PETITION cannot be filed in time to give statutory notice of the 4/5/21 hearing and to deliver courtesy copies to the Examiner by 3/26/21, the hearing will be continued again.

rulings.Case Title: SARA R. JULIAN IRREVOCABLE TRUST CREATED FOR THE BENEFIT OF ISAAC JULIAN BOOTS,DATED 1/16/18 EST. PURSUANT TO COURT
rulings.Calendar Matter: ACCOUNTING PETITION RE: Accounting #1, Report, Fees
rulings.Rulings: Continued to 3/29/2021. Issue: still no response to examiner notes. 1) Please provide legal authority for the account beginning 01/17/2019 and ending 02/29/2020. 2) Please explain the delay in funding the qualified tuition plan. 3) Page 5 line 9 of the petition alleges an ex parte petition for increase of bond will be filed under separate cover. Ex Parte has not been filed. [LRSF 14.14 B.] 4) On Schedule A-2 please explain the entry 02/24/2020 Sara Julian Trust Per order dated 12/20/19 $441,182.01. There appears to be no 12/20/19 order. The 12/20/2018 Order authorized $500,000.00 from the Sara R. Julian Trust to the Irrevocable Trust. Please explain why $441,182.01 + $25,000.00 was transferred and not $500,000.00. 5) On Schedule A-2 please explain the entry 02/28/2020 Sara Julian Trust Per order dated 12/20/20 $70,000.00. 6) In the 10/02/2020 Amendment Exhibit A please explain entry 06/27/2019 Reconciled client accounts for 2.50 hours.

rulings.Case Title: IN THE MATTER OF THE CANTILLANO IRREVOCABLE TRUST
rulings.Calendar Matter: Petition To Invalidate Irrevocable Trust, To Invalidate Trust Amendment, For Return Of Trust Property, For Intentional Interference
rulings.Rulings: Hearing. Appearance required. The Court to determine if notice is adequate and, if so, how best to proceed. Probate Code Section 850 requires notice with a copy of the petition to be given to a beneficiary of the trust, but any heir-at-law, who would stand to inherit, if the trust did not exist or were invalid, is a party whose right, title or interest would be affected by this petition, and, therefore, must receive (or waive) personal service of notice. [Prob. C. Sect. 17203(b) and CCP Sect. 413.10 et seq.] Notice has finally been mailed to the four other children of Juan Sabada, but they were not personally served. Since they are not beneficiaries under Juan Sabada's will, are they not interested parties in this proceeding? Due to the COVID-19 emergency, counsel and parties must, whenever possible, appear remotely through Zoom or CourtCall. Please review the 9th amended general order on the court's website for information on appearing remotely. For Zoom appearance: meeting ID is 875 8017 7925 and password is 722809. For audio-only telephone appearance via Zoom, call 1-669-900-6833 and follow the prompts, using the meeting ID and password above. PARTIES APPEARING SHOULD CHECK IN FOR THE HEARING USING THEIR FULL NAME.

For a video appearance, go to zoom.us, click "join a meeting" and input meeting ID 875 8017 7925 and password 722809. For an audio-only appearance through Zoom, call 1-669-900-6833 and key in meeting ID "875 8017 7925#", then participant ID "0#", then password "722809#". (Toll rates may apply).) The Court prefers that counsel and parties appear by video and that they input their first and last name into the "Your Name" dialogue box. Counsel and parties may appear by audio-only telephone through CourtCall by calling 1-888-882-6878. Please review the Court's 9th Amended General Order which took effect on 02/10/2021 for more information.
